context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=5; meal=bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=near; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected longer reheating for chicken and hash browns in that observed context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user again chose longer reheating for breaded fish bites and roasted potatoes, suggesting a tendency to want hotter savory meals in attention-demanding contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to microwave fruit-only items intended to be served cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected reheating for bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, indicating that front-TV meals do not imply no microwaving when the meal includes dessert items the user wants warmed.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user again prioritized preserving visibility toward the right side when attention to a right-side social partner likely mattered. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly selected preserving visibility toward the front while working. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected across multiple front-TV meals, indicating a stable preference to keep the front view clear.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN fast BECAUSE the user explicitly selected fast speed in that easy-to-serve, low-risk context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a more moderate speed for bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, suggesting the user slows the robot somewhat when the food is messier or bite acquisition is less trivial.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition during feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most solid foods, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly favored narrower bites that better match that constraint. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft dessert items such as brownies with sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in that observed meal, suggesting willingness to prioritize bite acquisition success for soft dessert foods even though the general tendency remains toward smaller bites.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose confirmation in the observed social meal, consistent with a preference for verifying bite acquisition when social smoothness and avoiding empty-fork transfers may matter. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again selected confirmation, suggesting a broader tendency to want explicit verification during attention-dividing contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that easier feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, indicating that in front-TV contexts the need for confirmation depends on bite-acquisition difficulty and messiness rather than the TV context alone.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and explicitly selected the less intrusive transfer style in the observed personal meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again selected the less intrusive transfer style, suggesting a stable preference for maintaining personal control over final approach. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user continued to prefer the less intrusive, user-controlled transfer style even in a task-focused context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again selected outside-mouth transfer across front-TV meals, reinforcing a stable cross-context preference.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance in the observed personal meal. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred a somewhat larger buffer in the observed social meal, suggesting more interpersonal comfort distance in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance while working, indicating comfort with efficient self-initiated reach when not managing interpersonal proximity. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN near BECAUSE the user consistently selected a close stop distance in front-TV meals.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user explicitly selected LED in the observed social meal, suggesting a preference for quieter, less socially disruptive signaling in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user again chose LED, consistent with a preference for low-disruption visual cues during focused front-facing activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a tendency to prefer quiet visual cues when attention is directed toward media.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves possible distraction or conversation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less attractive, and the observed social, TV, and laptop-working meals consistently favored explicit button control.

IF dining context involves possible distraction or conversation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and consistently preferred direct manual control in the observed meals, including social, TV, and laptop-working contexts.

IF dining context involves possible distraction or conversation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and consistently preferred direct manual control in the observed meals, including social, TV, and laptop-working contexts.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user explicitly selected LED in the observed social meal, suggesting a preference for quieter completion cues in social settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user again selected LED, indicating a preference for low-disruption completion cues during focused activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a preference for quiet visual completion cues in front-attention contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently chose the reliable, low-effort completion detection method.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling over automatic continuation.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling over automatic continuation.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user chose to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in a context where right-side visibility matters. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again prioritized reducing prolonged obstruction when attention to the right side was important. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retraction while working, indicating a stable preference to clear the visual workspace when front visibility matters. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a tendency to clear the line of sight when engaged with front-facing media.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND food_condition is simple items where dipping is optional, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly declined dipping in one observed front-TV meal despite sauce availability, showing a conservative baseline. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but not maximal dipping with tartar sauce, suggesting a moderate, mess-conscious preference in task-focused contexts. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a dessert meal with chocolate sauce, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping for bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, indicating that in front-TV contexts the user may allow moderate dipping when the sauce is integral to the meal. IF food_condition is a meal without dips or sauces AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary and the user's observed choices remain conservative.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly selected a very long wait in the observed social meal, indicating a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when social interaction may delay responses. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user again selected a very long wait, suggesting a stable preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided by front-facing work. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short wait in that easier front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that front-TV meal, indicating that in TV contexts the wait preference depends on meal complexity, with more intervention time preferred for messier or less trivial foods.
